items.title: Meet the finalists in the 2026 European Inventor Award
items.title_zh: EPO 公布 2026 年欧洲发明人奖 12 名入围者
items.title_en: EPO Announces Twelve Finalists for the 2026 European Inventor Award
items.summary: Twelve finalists for the 2026 European Inventor Award span biotechnology, healthcare, renewable energy, semiconductors, digital tech and quantum. Winners will be revealed on 2 July.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局公布 2026 年欧洲发明人奖 12 名入围者，技术领域覆盖生物技术、医疗健康、可再生能源、半导体、数字技术、食品技术、量子、铁路和先进制造。获奖结果将于 2026 年 7 月 2 日在柏林线上线下颁奖活动中揭晓，公众奖投票已开放。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has revealed twelve finalists for the 2026 European Inventor Award, spanning biotechnology, healthcare, renewable energy, semiconductors, digital technology, and quantum computing. The finalists represent innovations in areas such as malaria vaccines, smart battery recycling, and nanofibre manufacturing. Public voting for the Popular Prize is now open, allowing three votes per day until the ceremony on 2 July in Berlin. The Popular Prize winner will be determined by a combination of jury votes and public participation. Winners in the Industry, Research, and Non-EPO Countries categories will also be announced during the event. The Lifetime Achievement Award recipient will be disclosed separately on 10 June.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该奖项本身不是规则变化，但可作为观察 EPO 关注技术方向和欧洲创新叙事的窗口。半导体、数字存储、量子和可再生能源等入围领域与欧洲产业政策和专利布局热点高度相关。
items.ai_insight_en: This announcement highlights technological priorities aligned with European industrial policy, particularly in green tech and digital sovereignty. For IP professionals, the finalist list offers a benchmark for high-value patent portfolios in emerging fields. Monitoring these innovations provides insight into competitive landscapes and potential licensing opportunities. The public voting mechanism also serves as a notable engagement tool for rights holders seeking visibility.

items.title: G 1/25: Oral proceedings before the Enlarged Board of Appeal on description adaptation - GJE
items.title_zh: G 1/25案口头审理：欧洲专利说明书适应性修改
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board Holds Oral Proceedings in G 1/25 on Description Adaptation
items.summary: Oral proceedings in G 1/25 (“Hydroponics”) took place today before the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBA) at the European Patent Office (EPO)
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会今日就G 1/25号案件（“Hydroponics”）举行口头审理。该案源于T 697/22号决定，核心争议在于为符合《欧洲专利公约》，欧洲专利的说明书是否必须以及如何根据修改后的权利要求进行适应性调整。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office’s Enlarged Board of Appeal conducted oral proceedings today in case G 1/25, commonly referred to as “Hydroponics.” This referral originates from decision T 697/22 and addresses a critical procedural question: whether and under what legal basis the description of a European patent application must be adapted to align with amended claims. The core issue concerns compliance with the European Patent Convention regarding consistency between claims and description. These proceedings aim to clarify the mandatory standards for description adaptation during examination. The outcome will provide essential guidance on the legal requirements for ensuring that the description adequately supports the claimed subject matter, significantly impacting future patent prosecution strategies and drafting practices before the EPO.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案旨在统一欧洲专利实践中关于说明书与权利要求一致性的审查标准。实务中需密切关注扩大上诉委员会的最终决定，以明确在审查及异议程序中修改权利要求时，对说明书进行适应性修改的法律义务边界及具体操作要求。
items.ai_insight_en: This development carries significant implications for patent applicants and rights holders prosecuting cases before the EPO. A stricter interpretation of description adaptation requirements could necessitate more robust initial drafting to ensure full support for potential claim amendments. Applicants should anticipate heightened scrutiny regarding the alignment between descriptive text and claimed subject matter. Proactively addressing these consistency issues during the application phase may mitigate risks of later objections or limitations on protection scope. Ensuring high-quality, comprehensive descriptions now can safeguard against procedural hurdles and enhance the stability of granted patents across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会今日对G 1/25案进行口头审理，聚焦说明书修改问题", "案件源于T 697/22，探讨说明书是否需随权利要求修改而调整以合规"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EPO对说明书适应性修改义务的最终法律界定", "监测该决定对未来欧洲专利申请及确权程序中撰写策略的影响", "等待扩大上诉委员会发布正式书面决定以获取详细裁判理由"]

items.title: France Design Impact Award 2026
items.title_zh: 法国国家工业产权局公布2026年设计影响力奖40强项目
items.title_en: INPI Reveals 40 Finalists for France Design Impact Award 2026
items.summary: France Design Impact Award 2026 sgurtler@inpi.fr 07 mai 2026 Les 40 projets du prix France Design Impact award 2026 ont été officiellement révélés lors de l’inauguration de la Foire économique de Tours le 2 mai. Cette sélection 2026 met en avant des initiatives venues de toutes les régions de France et démontre le rôle concret du design face aux grands enjeux climatiques et politiques. À travers différentes scènes de vie quotidienne (se soigner, habiter, se déplacer, s’instruire, communiquer, pr
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）于5月2日在图尔经济博览会开幕仪式上正式揭晓“2026法国设计影响力奖”的40个入围项目。该评选由经济、环境和社会三个专家陪审团共同评估，旨在展示设计在应对气候与政治挑战中的具体作用。INPI作为历史合作伙伴参与经济陪审团，强调设计作为经济转型战略杠杆的价值，涵盖产品、服务及品牌视觉等多个领域，凸显了外观设计在商业生态中的核心地位。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) officially announced the 40 finalists for the 2026 France Design Impact Award during the inauguration of the Tours Economic Fair on May 2. The selection highlights initiatives from across all French regions, demonstrating design's concrete role in addressing major climatic and political challenges. Covering daily life scenarios such as healthcare, housing, mobility, education, communication, production, and communal living, the award underscores design as a strategic lever for economic transformation. The projects span product, industrial, brand, visual identity, user interface, spatial, and service design.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视外观设计在欧盟市场的战略价值，不仅限于注册保护，更需结合ESG理念提升设计竞争力。建议关注INPI等机构的设计激励政策，通过高质量设计增强品牌溢价，并积极参与相关国际奖项以提升在欧洲市场的知名度与合规形象。
items.ai_insight_en: This initiative highlights the growing intersection between intellectual property strategy and sustainability metrics within the European design landscape. For rights holders, participation in or monitoring of such awards provides visibility into how national offices value non-traditional IP assets like service and interface design. It signals that commercial impact and ecological vision are increasingly central to official recognition frameworks.

items.title: Tuesday at INTA: How AI is assisting judges | IP’s role in deals
items.title_zh: INTA焦点：AI如何辅助法官裁判及IP在交易中的角色
items.title_en: UK Judges Discuss AI Use in Judgments and IP Role in Corporate Deals at INTA
items.summary: Senior UK judges discussing the impact of AI on the judiciary, and the role of in-house IP lawyers during corporate transactions and carve-outs were among the top talking points
items.ai_summary_zh: 在INTA年会上，英格兰和威尔士高等法院及上诉法院法官讨论了AI在司法中的应用。上诉法院法官Colin Birss透露，他使用AI进行判决的逻辑一致性检查，以发现段落间的矛盾或需重写的部分，但明确反对用AI从头生成判决书。 panel还包括UKIPO指定人员Emma Himsworth。此外，会议还探讨了企业内部IP律师在公司剥离交易中的作用。这反映了英国司法界对AI工具持谨慎辅助态度，强调人类法官的最终责任。
items.ai_summary_en: Senior judges from the England and Wales High Court and Court of Appeal addressed the impact of artificial intelligence on the judiciary during a panel at the INTA Annual Meeting. Lord Justice Colin Birss, alongside Mr Justices Richard Meade and James Mellor, discussed practical applications of AI tools. Lord Justice Birss revealed he uses AI to sense-check judgments for inconsistencies, though he rejects using it to draft decisions from scratch. The session also covered the evolving role of in-house IP counsel in corporate transactions and carve-outs, moderated by Arty Rajendra of Osborne Clarke.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This development signals a cautious but growing integration of AI assistance within the English judiciary, specifically for quality control rather than decision-making. For rights holders and litigants, this suggests that judicial reasoning may become more internally consistent due to automated checks. It underscores the continued necessity for precise legal drafting and clear argumentation, as AI tools may highlight ambiguities that judges previously overlooked or resolved manually.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国法官利用AI进行判决逻辑一致性检查，辅助识别潜在矛盾", "法官明确拒绝使用AI从头生成判决书，坚持人类主导裁判", "会议同时探讨了内部IP律师在企业并购与资产剥离中的关键作用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Lord Justice Birss uses AI to identify logical inconsistencies in drafted judgments but maintains human authorship.", "The panel included High Court justices and a UKIPO appointed person discussing judicial technology adoption.", "Discussions extended to the strategic importance of in-house IP lawyers during complex corporate deals."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["适应英国司法AI辅助趋势，强化法律文书逻辑严密性以应对智能审查", "重视IP在商业交易中的合规价值，借助专业力量规避法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants should ensure legal submissions are logically coherent, as judges increasingly use AI to detect contradictions.", "Practice note: Monitor how UK courts define the boundaries of AI assistance to understand potential shifts in judgment clarity standards.", "What to monitor: Future guidance from the UK judiciary on acceptable AI tools and protocols for legal professionals interacting with the court."]
core_points_en: Lord Justice Birss uses AI to identify logical inconsistencies in drafted judgments but maintains human authorship.
The panel included High Court justices and a UKIPO appointed person discussing judicial technology adoption.
Discussions extended to the strategic importance of in-house IP lawyers during complex corporate deals.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigants should ensure legal submissions are logically coherent, as judges increasingly use AI to detect contradictions.
Practice note: Monitor how UK courts define the boundaries of AI assistance to understand potential shifts in judgment clarity standards.
What to monitor: Future guidance from the UK judiciary on acceptable AI tools and protocols for legal professionals interacting with the court.

The campaign emphasizes how IP protection supports advancements in athletic equipment, performance-enhancing technologies, and broadcast media. To mark the occasion, the EPO is collaborating with national IP offices to launch a coordinated social media campaign. Participants are encouraged to follow the conversation using the #WorldIPDay hashtag to discover stories illustrating how IP motivates athletes and connects fans worldwide.

items.ai_summary_en: This report highlights key discussions from the two-day Standard Essential Patent (SEP) Summit held in Hong Kong. The conference addressed significant shifts in industry mindsets regarding licensing negotiations and examined the evolving role and future trajectory of patent pools within the global ecosystem. A central focus was placed on debates surrounding Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) mechanisms as viable alternatives to prolonged litigation. Notably, insights provided by a Unified Patent Court (UPC) judge offered critical perspectives on judicial approaches to SEP disputes in Europe. Additionally, the strategic licensing posture of Oppo was analyzed to illustrate practical applications of these concepts. Although situated in Asia, the proceedings reflected broader global trends impacting European markets and judicial practices, providing essential context for understanding the current complex landscape of SEP licensing and enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极度匮乏，无法评估UPC法官的具体观点或Oppo策略细节。需关注后续是否有会议实录发布，以了解欧洲司法机构对亚洲SEP许可市场的看法及临时许可机制的最新动向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor Unified Patent Court judicial tendencies, particularly regarding interim relief and licensing frameworks. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for optimizing FRAND compliance and negotiating interim licences effectively. Companies are advised to consider Alternative Dispute Resolution mechanisms to mitigate litigation costs and manage procedural risks efficiently. Furthermore, tracking developments in patent pool structures remains vital for navigating the increasingly intricate global SEP environment. Aligning internal strategies with these judicial and market trends will help stakeholders protect their interests while maintaining robust compliance standards across jurisdictions.

items.title: Smarter Brand Protection: AI, Action And ROI

items.title_en: Smarter Brand Protection: Leveraging AI, Enforcement Actions, and ROI Strategies
items.summary: Counterfeiting and online brand abuse are no longer isolated challenges — they are global, fast-moving, and increasingly sophisticated. From social media and marketplaces to offline distribution networks, brands today must navigate a complex and constantly evolving threat landscape.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文介绍一场关于品牌保护的研讨会，聚焦欧盟及法国市场。内容涵盖利用AI技术检测在线假冒和商标侵权，结合线下调查与法律程序进行执法，以及如何优化预算以实现投资回报最大化。案例涉及Vivienne Westwood品牌，旨在提供可立即应用的最佳实践。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis addresses the escalating complexity of global counterfeiting and online brand abuse, exploring how artificial intelligence can transform brand protection strategies. It covers omnichannel monitoring spanning social media platforms to offline distribution networks, emphasizing the use of automated tools to enhance infringement detection efficiency. The content guides rights holders on balancing online and offline enforcement resources to maximize return on investment. Ultimately, it aims to reposition brand protection from a reactive cost center into a strategic, data-driven function capable of navigating sophisticated digital threats and protecting brand integrity across diverse markets.

Quelles nouvelles influences sur la propriété intellectuelle ? », le forum, devenu au fil des ans incontournable et reconnu à l’échelle internationale, a une nouvelle fois réuni des intervenants de renom devant un parterre de dirigeants de PME et de prof
items.ai_summary_zh: 由 Unifab 组织的 2026 年欧洲知识产权论坛在巴黎举行，聚焦地缘政治、立法及技术对知识产权的新影响。法国工业产权局（INPI）作为历史合作伙伴参会，总局长 Pascal Faure 强调需协助企业将无形资产保护纳入发展战略，以培育欧洲冠军企业，并探讨了优化知识产权获取路径的潜在改革方向。
items.ai_summary_en: Organized by UNIFAB, the 2026 European Intellectual Property Forum (FEPI) convened in Paris to address how geopolitical shifts, legislative changes, and technological advancements influence intellectual property protection. The event featured seven panel discussions focusing on global anti-counterfeiting efforts and cross-border innovation practices. Pascal Faure, Director General of the French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI), emphasized the critical need for companies to integrate intangible asset protection into their core development strategies. He highlighted INPI's ongoing commitment to supporting businesses and exploring reforms to facilitate easier access to IP systems, reinforcing the institute's role in fostering European champions through robust IP valorization.

Stakeholders should note the institutional focus on facilitating access to IP mechanisms, which may signal upcoming administrative or procedural adjustments in France and potentially across the EU.

items.ai_summary_en: The Academy of European Law (ERA) will host its Annual Conference on European Media Law in Trier, Germany, and online on 7–8 May 2026. The conference aims to explore these latest legislative developments and facilitate discussion on the coordination of media and intellectual property policies at the EU level. IPKat readers are eligible for a specific discount to attend this hybrid event, providing an opportunity to engage directly with experts on the evolving regulatory landscape affecting digital content and platform responsibilities across Europe.

Via’s president noted that while explaining the complex mechanics of patent pools to the general public remains challenging, high-tech rights holders have fully embraced their strategic value. The gathering underscores the vibrant dynamics within the European technology market regarding patent licensing and portfolio management. It highlights a growing industry consensus on the effectiveness of standardized licensing models, reflecting increased engagement from key market participants in navigating intellectual property frameworks and fostering collaborative approaches to technology commercialization across borders.

items.title: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.title_zh: 雅典展示IPEP：以数字工具强化知识产权保护
items.title_en: EUIPO Presents IPEP Digital Tools to Strengthen IP Enforcement in Greece
items.summary: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月27日，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）在希腊雅典支持举办现场执法人员培训。活动由希腊市场控制局与全球反假冒集团共同组织，旨在通过IPEP及其移动应用提升一线官员识别假冒商品能力。约15家权利人企业分享汽配、时尚及烟草领域的打假实务，促进当局与权利人间的实时信息交换与合作。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO presented its IP Enforcement Portal (IPEP) during a training session for frontline officers in Athens on 27 February 2026. Co-organized by Greek market control authorities and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group, the event targeted customs and police officials responsible for detecting counterfeit goods. Approximately fifteen rights holders shared practical guidance on identifying fakes in sectors such as automotive parts, fashion, and tobacco. The EUIPO showcased IPEP’s capabilities for secure, real-time information exchange and introduced the IPEP Mobile App, which enables multilingual rights searches and suspicious case notifications. This initiative aims to enhance cooperation between national authorities and industry stakeholders through digital enforcement tools.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This event highlights the EUIPO’s strategy to digitize enforcement cooperation at the national level. For rights holders, active participation in such training offers a channel to educate customs and police on specific product identifiers. The deployment of the IPEP Mobile App suggests a shift toward faster, field-based verification processes. Companies operating in Greece should monitor the adoption of these digital tools by local authorities, as effective use can streamline detention procedures and reduce the time required for initial infringement assessments.

items.summary: The IPKat blog reports on copyright, patent, trade mark, info-tech and confidentiality issues from a mainly UK and European perspective.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国特许专利代理人协会（CIPA）正为其权威指南《专利法黑皮书》最新版招募作者，重点涵盖侵权诉讼、专利撤销及统一专利法院（UPC）实施等章节。同时，编辑团队向业界征集内容修订建议，旨在确保该指南准确反映包括单一专利制度在内的最新法律动态，以满足专业需求。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注此指南更新，因其深刻影响在英及通过 UPC 的专利策略。建议利用反馈渠道表达诉求，并密切跟踪新版中关于统一专利法院的实务指引，以优化在欧洲的专利布局与风险应对。

items.title: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.title_zh: 强化打击有组织犯罪：EUIPO举办EMPACT通讯网络会议
items.title_en: EUIPO Hosts EMPACT Communicators Network to Amplify Fight Against Organised Crime
items.summary: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局在阿利坎特主办欧盟打击刑事威胁多学科平台（EMPACT）通讯网络会议。来自欧盟各地的传播专家齐聚，旨在加强向公众解释打击严重和有组织犯罪的联合行动。会议聚焦于提升EMPACT作为欧盟执法合作基石的可见度，协调信息并分享最佳实践，以支持2026-2029新运营周期的政治势头和公众信心。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office hosted the EMPACT Communicators Network in Alicante, gathering communication experts to strengthen public messaging on joint actions against serious and organised crime. As EMPACT enters its 2026–2029 operational cycle, participants focused on sustaining political momentum, supporting operational excellence, and reinforcing public confidence. The network aims to align messages and share best practices to enhance the visibility of EU law enforcement cooperation. EMPACT serves as the EU’s flagship framework for coordinating investigations into threats such as trafficking, fraud, cybercrime, and intellectual property crime, ensuring operational achievements are understood beyond specialist audiences.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次会议侧重于执法合作的战略传播与公众认知，而非具体的法律程序或判例更新。实务中应关注EMPACT框架下针对知识产权犯罪的跨境执法趋势，以及欧盟机构如何通过统一叙事强化对内部市场保护的公众支持。这反映了欧盟层面将IP执法纳入更广泛的安全与犯罪治理议程的政策导向。
items.ai_insight_en: This event underscores the integration of IP enforcement within broader EU security frameworks. For rights holders, it signals continued institutional priority on combating organised IP crime through coordinated multi-agency action. Monitoring EMPACT’s communication strategy offers insight into how EU agencies plan to publicize enforcement results and maintain political support for internal market protection measures during the new operational cycle.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EMPACT框架下知识产权犯罪执法的跨机构协作动态及优先事项变化", "观察欧盟机构如何通过战略传播提升公众对IP执法行动的理解与支持", "监测2026-2029周期内针对有组织犯罪中IP侵权环节的联合行动成果"]
